title: Factor / factored form
short: Writing an expression as a product, like x² − 4 = (x − 2)(x + 2).
full:
To factor an expression is to rewrite it as a product of simpler factors: x² − 4 = (x − 2)(x + 2). It is the reverse of expanding (multiplying out).

Factoring is powerful for solving equations: once a polynomial is a product of factors equal to zero, each factor can be set to zero in turn, immediately giving the roots. The factored form also reveals an expression's roots at a glance.

It is a pure-algebra operation with no units.
